title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Go Client Systems · Runtime Recall
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for one-shot bodies, streaming, uploads, decompression, and resource limits, producing bounded streaming body pipeline. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http bounded streaming body pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go One Shot Body Ownership · changed case · Review One Shot Body Ownership
steps.instruction:
review case — Go security handoff client streams a 20 MiB upload and receives a 40 KiB compressed body that expands past 50 MiB. Define one-shot ownership, replay, compressed and decoded limits, cancellation, trailers, and cleanup. Competency probe: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
